data.title: Fitch Assigns Autonoria DE 2025 Final Ratings
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-Frankfurt am Main-27 November 2025:

Fitch Ratings has assigned Autonoria DE 2025 final ratings, as detailed below.

The final ratings assigned to the class D to F notes are one notch above the expected ratings due to the lower-than-anticipated overall funding costs of the transaction.

Autonoria DE 2025

----A FR0014013Z63; Long Term Rating; New Rating; AAAsf; Rating Outlook Stable

----B FR0014013Z48; Long Term Rating; New Rating; AA-sf; Rating Outlook Stable

----C FR0014013ZA4; Long Term Rating; New Rating; Asf; Rating Outlook Stable

----D FR0014013Z71; Long Term Rating; New Rating; BBB+sf; Rating Outlook Stable

----E FR0014013ZE6; Long Term Rating; New Rating; BBBsf; Rating Outlook Stable

----F FR0014013Z89; Long Term Rating; New Rating; BB+sf; Rating Outlook Stable

----G FR0014013Z97; Long Term Rating; New Rating; NRsf

Transaction Summary

Autonoria DE 2025 is a securitisation of auto loans, with balloon or fully amortising, originated by BNP Paribas SA (A+/Stable/F1), German Branch (trading name: Consors Finanz). Borrowers are mostly employed, but there are some self-employed and retired obligors in the pool. About half of the portfolio balance consists of loans to finance leisure and recreational vehicles.

The transaction has a 13-month revolving period. The class A to G notes will then pay down according to a target over-collateralisation (OC) mechanism until a performance or another trigger is breached.

KEY RATING DRIVERS

Diverging Sub-Pool Defaults: Fitch applies different default base cases for passenger car and leisure vehicle loans, at 2.25% and 1%, respectively, because of diverging historical asset performance. The multiples at 'AAA' are 5.5x and 6.5x, while the recovery base case at 50% and 'AAA' haircut at 45% are the same for both pools. These assumptions reflect our expectation of moderate growth recovery and a still robust, although cooling, labour market. We have not assumed a significant change in pool composition during the revolving period.

Target OC Allocations: The class A to G notes will amortise according to target OC (TOC) percentages, equal to the OC at closing. The allocation dynamics are a driving factor of our cash flow analysis as in the main rating scenarios the TOC will apply for 14 months (AAA) up to 36 months (BB+). As a result of the TOC allocations, in our 'AAA' scenario, payments to the class B to G notes will be around 4% of the initial asset balance, resulting in limited accumulation of credit enhancement for the class A notes.

Excess Spread Adds Protection: The weighted average interest rate of the closing pool is around 5%, outweighing the issuer's costs. This creates excess spread, which can compensate for defaults through principal deficiency ledgers. A replenishment limit on the minimum average interest rate of the additional purchased receivables ensures that the excess spread will not significantly change during the revolving period.

Counterparty Risks Addressed: The transaction has a fully funded liquidity reserve for payment interruption scenarios. There are also reserves for commingling and set-off risk, which will be funded if the seller is downgraded below 'BBB' and 'F2'. All these reserves are adequate to cover the relevant exposures, in our view. Rating triggers and remedial actions for the account bank and swap counterparty are adequately defined and in line with our criteria.

RATING SENSITIVITIES Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

Expected impact on the notes' ratings of increased defaults (class A/B/C/D/E/F)

Increase default rates by 10%: 'AA+sf'/'A+sf'/'A-sf'/'BBBsf'/'BBB-sf'/'BB+sf'

Increase default rates by 25%: 'AA+sf'/'A+sf'/'BBB+sf'/'BBB-sf'/'BBB-sf'/'BB+sf'

Increase default rates by 50%: 'AA-sf'/'A-sf'/'BBBsf'/'BB+sf'/'BB+sf'/'BBsf'

Expected impact on the notes' ratings of reduced recoveries (class A/B/C/D/E/F)

Reduce recovery rates by 10%: 'AA+sf'/'AA-sf'/'A-sf'/'BBBsf'/'BBB-sf'/'BB+sf'

Reduce recovery rates by 25%: 'AA+sf'/'A+sf'/'BBB+sf'/'BBB-sf'/'BB+sf'/'BBsf'

Reduce recovery rates by 50%: 'AAsf'/'Asf'/'BBBsf'/'BB+sf'/'BBsf'/'BB-sf'

Expected impact on the notes' ratings of increased defaults and reduced recoveries (class A/B/C/D/E/F)

Increase default rates by 10% and reduce recovery rates by 10%: 'AA+sf'/'A+sf'/'A-sf'/'BBBsf'/'BBB-sf'/'BB+sf'

Increase default rates by 25% and reduce recovery rates by 25%: 'AAsf'/'A-sf'/'BBBsf'/'BB+sf'/'BBsf'/'BB-sf'

Increase default rates by 50% and reduce recovery rates by 50%: 'Asf'/'BBBsf'/'BB+sf'/'BB-sf'/'B+sf'/'Bsf'

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

Unanticipated decreases in the frequency of defaults or increases in recovery rates could produce smaller losses than the base case and result in positive rating action on the notes.

Expected impact on the notes' ratings of decreased default rates and increased recoveries (class A/B/C/D/E/F)

Decrease default rates by 10% and increase recovery rates by 10%: 'AAAsf'/'AAsf'/'Asf'/'BBB+sf'/'BBB+sf'/'BBB-sf'.

USE OF THIRD PARTY DUE DILIGENCE PURSUANT TO SEC RULE 17G -10

Form ABS Due Diligence-15E was not provided to, or reviewed by, Fitch in relation to this rating action.

DATA ADEQUACY

Fitch reviewed the results of a third party assessment conducted on the asset portfolio information, and concluded that there were no findings that affected the rating analysis.

Overall, and together with any assumptions referred to above, Fitch's assessment of the information relied upon for the agency's rating analysis according to its applicable rating methodologies indicates that it is adequately reliable.

REFERENCES FOR SUBSTANTIALLY MATERIAL SOURCE CITED AS KEY DRIVER OF RATING The principal sources of information used in the analysis are described in the Applicable Criteria. REPRESENTATIONS, WARRANTIES AND ENFORCEMENT MECHANISMS A description of the transaction's representations, warranties and enforcement mechanisms (RW&Es) that are disclosed in the offering document and which relate to the underlying asset pool is available by clicking the link to the Appendix. The appendix also contains a comparison of these RW&Es to those Fitch considers typical for the asset class as detailed in the Special Report titled 'Representations, Warranties and Enforcement Mechanisms in Global Structured Finance Transactions'.

ESG Considerations

The highest level of ESG credit relevance is a score of '3', unless otherwise disclosed in this section. A score of '3' means ESG issues are credit-neutral or have only a minimal credit impact on the entity, either due to their nature or the way in which they are being managed by the entity. data.title: UK gilt yields edge up a day after post-budget bond rally
data.description:
British government bond yields rose modestly on Thursday, mostly for medium-dated maturities, reversing some of their sharp rally the previous day after finance minister Rachel Reeves' budget announcement and issuance plans of the Debt Management Office.

The 10-year gilt yield (GB10YT=RR) was up 3 basis points at 1430 GMT at 4.454%, after a 7 bps drop on Wednesday.

Long-dated bond yields rose by the least, which investors attributed to a reduction in the DMO's plans to sell such bonds, which are more expensive for the government to finance.

BOOSTING APPEAL OF LONG-DATED GILTS

The 30-year yield (GB30YT=RR) was flat on the day at 5.215%, after plunging by 12 bps on Wednesday - the biggest drop since April - after the DMO said it had cancelled three auctions of long-dated gilts.

"Its been all down to the prudence of the Debt Management Office," Craig Inches, head of rates and cash at Royal London Asset Management, said, regarding the interest from investors in long-dated gilts.

The auction cancellations meant it would be months until more long-dated gilts were sold, adding to their appeal, he said.

The DMO revised up its 2025/26 gilt issuance by 4.6 billion pounds ($6.1 billion) to 303.7 billion pounds after Reeves' annual budget on Wednesday, a slightly smaller increase than the median 8.6 billion-pound rise predicted in a Reuters poll of gilt dealers published on Tuesday.

Investors took comfort from the bigger room for error that Reeves now has for meeting her fiscal targets after her budget announcement, something which could reduce the speculation about the public finances that has weighed on the market for months.

"We see room for gilts to continue pricing out any elevated political risk and term premia built up recently," economists and strategists from BNP Paribas said in a note. "The fiscal consolidation pencilled in for the next fiscal year should also support the trade for now."

Others say the outlook for gilts next year remains cloudy.

Francis Diamond, JPMorgan's head of European rates strategy research, told Reuters that while the budget reduces near-term uncertainty, it did not change the bank's expectations for gilt yields to rise next year.

data.title: ARA gasoil stocks fall 7%, Insights Global data shows
data.description:
Gasoil stocks independently held in the Amsterdam-Rotterdam-Antwerp (ARA) refining and storage hub fell by 7% on the week, data from Dutch consultancy Insights Global showed on Thursday.

  • Gasoil stocks, which include diesel, were 7% lower at 2.02 million tons on higher inland demand for heating oil, Insights Global's Lars van Wageningen said.

  • Gasoline stocks rose 10% to 1.08 million tons on slower exports, Wageningen said, adding that exports to west Africa could pick up in the coming days.

  • Jet fuel stocks fell by nearly 4% to 1.05 million tons on lower imports.

  • Fuel oil stocks declined 9% to 1.01 million tons on export demand to the Mediterranean, Wageningen said.

data.title: Fitch Upgrades Salik Company PJSC to 'A'; Outlook Stable
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-Dubai-27 November 2025:

Fitch Ratings has upgraded Salik Company PJSC's Long-Term Issuer Default Rating (IDR) to 'A', from 'A-'. The Outlook is Stable. We have also revised its Standalone Credit Profile (SCP) to 'a', from 'a-'.

RATING RATIONALE

The upgrade follows an improvement in the government of Dubai's credit quality, given Salik's strong ties to the state. Salik is 75% indirectly owned by Dubai, which oversees its activities. Salik is a major state-owned entity that would receive government support.

Salik's 'a' SCP considers its exclusive position in Dubai's toll road system, low leverage and long concession agreement (49 years), which ensures stability of long-term cash flow generation. The SCP also considers the company's reliance on the domestic banking system, exposure to the emirate's economic environment and its multiple ties with the government of Dubai, the controlling shareholder, regulator and approver of yearly tariff rises.

KEY RATING DRIVERS

Decision-Making and Oversight - 'Very Strong'

Salik is primarily owned by Dubai Investment Fund (DIF), which holds 75.1% of the company's shares and is ultimately owned and controlled by the government of Dubai. The government, through its representation on Salik's board, oversees and controls the company's activities. The state's concession agreement with Salik provides the framework under which the toll road operates. The Road and Transport Authority (RTA) is the regulator, while the Dubai Executive Counsel (TEC) ultimately makes the decision on tariffs and all major events. Fitch does not expect changes to Salik's majority ownership.

Precedents of Support - 'Strong'

The government's majority ownership creates strong alignment of interests among all stakeholders within Salik's ecosystem, including Dubai Executive Counsel, RTA, and DIF. This alignment underscores the importance of maintaining Salik's financial stability to achieve the Dubai city's long-term traffic goals. This was evident when Salik successfully negotiated favourable terms for two additional gates, including the upfront payment of fees over six years without interest, reducing the need for immediate funding from the company's balance sheet.

Preservation of Government Policy Role - 'Strong'

Salik is pivotal in improving Dubai's traffic management and fostering the city's tourism and economic growth, in line with the government's strategic objectives. Any interruption or delays in the company's operations could disrupt traffic in major areas and impede progress towards achieving these goals.

Contagion Risk - 'Strong'

Salik is present in the local debt market, through the banking system. A default on its debt obligations would likely have a negative impact on the reputation of the government and other government-related entities (GRE), affecting their ability to secure funding from debt markets. This is due to the company's close connection with the government through the DIF, which also holds large stakes in other major government-controlled entities.

Revenue Risk Volume: 'Stronger'

Robust Traffic Volume, Strategic Positioning: Salik benefits, as the exclusive toll operator in Dubai for existing and new gates under its concession agreement with RTA, from the city's expansion and population growth. Toll revenue, which constitutes 87% of Salik's income, is heavily dependent on traffic volumes, closely tied to Dubai's economy and population growth. Salik's open road tolling system currently includes 10 gates. Other revenue streams are Salik's tag activation fees, fines and penalties (about 11%) or barrier-free parking payment solutions. The peak-to-trough variation in traffic volumes remains among the lowest in Fitch's EMEA portfolio, at about 2%.

Revenue Risk Price: 'Midrange'

Inflation-Linked Tariff Adjustment: Salik can propose tariff increases linked to inflation under the concession agreement. The Dubai Executive Counsel has the final decision on these adjustments and, if a proposed increase is rejected, the company is compensated through a reduction in the annual concession fee paid to RTA. This fee adjustment is capped and floored, as the fees cannot be higher than 25% or lower than 15% of toll revenue. In 2024, the concession fee was reduced to 22.5%, from 25%, as Salik's tariffs remained flat. The mechanism offers good but not full protection from political interference, as concession fees cannot be reduced if they hit the floor.

Salik implemented variable pricing in January 2025, introducing peak (AED6), off-peak (AED4), and past-midnight (AED0) tariffs, replacing the previously flat AED4 tariff.

Infrastructure Development and Renewal: 'Stronger'

Capex Absorbed by RTA: Salik's infrastructure development risk is mitigated by its capex-light business model. Under the concession agreement, RTA is responsible for the development and maintenance of the associated road network infrastructure, bearing all related costs. The company has the exclusive right to undertake tolling works for new toll gates and is reimbursed by RTA for construction costs on a cost-plus-10% basis. For the replacement of tolling assets at the end of their useful life, it is reimbursed on a cost-plus-5% basis. Salik's responsibilities are limited to the operation and maintenance of the modern, low-maintenance toll gates.

Debt Structure: 'Midrange'

Bullet Debt, Some Covenants: The debt is senior unsecured, with some covenants but lacks a security package. It has a bullet payment structure and a floating interest rate with no hedging in place and matures in 2027. Salik also has a committed undrawn revolving credit facility maturing in 2027. Its term loan was mainly used to finance the upfront concession payment to RTA and for general corporate purposes. Refinancing risk is reduced by the company's robust financial standing and potential government support. The term loan includes an event of default and a dividend lock-up provision at a leverage ratio of 5x on a post-IFRS basis.

Financial Profile

The Fitch base case projects average net debt/EBITDA of 1.8x for 2025-2030, while the Fitch rating case projects average net debt/EBITDA of 1.9x.

Under our rating case, Fitch-calculated net leverage peaks at 2.3x in 2025 due to the upfront concession fee liability to RTA related to the new two toll gates, before gradually declining to 1.4x by end-2029 as annual upfront fee payments to RTA are settled.

PEER GROUP

GRE Peers

Salik's closest GRE peer in the GIG portfolio is Abu Dhabi Ports Company PJSC (ADP, AA-/Stable), which we also rate under our Transportation and GRE criteria. Our assessment of both GREs results in a support score of 35. However, the difference in rating reflects each GRE's SCP and Fitch's assessment of the government's credit quality.

Toll Road Peers

Salik's main peers are the European toll road networks of APRR (A/Stable) and Vinci S.A. (A-/Stable). All three have similar peak-to-trough metrics (between 2% and 3%) and business risk profiles. Salik's average leverage is lower at about 1.9x, versus Vinci's 2.5x under the Fitch rating case, while APRR has higher average leverage of about 3.5x. Nevertheless, Salik and APRR are rated at the same level, while Vinci is rated lower than both.

Salik's SCP is linked to the credit quality of the government of Dubai, and Vinci's rating reflects the greater volatility of its contracting business.

RATING SENSITIVITIES Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

- A deterioration in the government of Dubai's credit quality

- Failure to refinance the term loan in a timely manner or refinancing the loan at far higher interest rates

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

- An improvement in the government of Dubai's credit quality

The highest level of ESG credit relevance is a score of '3', unless otherwise disclosed in this section. A score of '3' means ESG issues are credit neutral or have only a minimal credit impact on the entity, either due to their nature or the way in which they are being managed by the entity. Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores are not inputs in the rating process; they are an observation on the relevance and materiality of ESG factors in the rating decision. For more information on Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores, visit https://www.fitchratings.com/topics/esg/products#esg-relevance-scores.

data.title: TotalEnergies, Repsol in Talks to Combine U.K. North Sea Assets, Cinco Dias Says, Citing Sources
data.description:
• France's TotalEnergies and Spain's Repsol are in advanced talks to combine their oil and gas exploration and production businesses in the U.K. North Sea, Spanish business newspaper Cinco Dias reports, citing unnamed sources.
  • Repsol recently established a U.K. joint venture with Neo Energy, and TotalEnergies could join the business by contributing some of its assets, Cinco Dias reports.
  • TotalEnergies and Repsol declined to comment to Cinco Dias.

Full story (in Spanish): https://tinyurl.com/3tjt5286

SALE OF ORDINARY SHARES FROM TREASURY

The Board of CT UK High Income Trust PLC announces that on 27 November 2025 the Company sold from treasury 550,000 Ordinary shares at a price of 110.69p per Ordinary share.

Following this transaction the Company's issued share capital consists of 102,067,144 Ordinary shares and 32,076,703 B shares, of which 15,044,491 Ordinary shares and 1,742,953 B shares are held in treasury.  Therefore the total number of shares with voting rights in CT UK High Income Trust PLC is 87,022,653 Ordinary shares and 30,333,750 B shares.

Board member, Jan Juul, has today informed the board of directors that he due to change of job position wishes to resign from the board of directors in Agillic A/S as of 27 November 2025.

data.title: Elopak ASA: Issuance of new 5-year green bonds
data.description:
Elopak ASA (“Elopak”, ticker: ELO), rated BBB-/Stable by Nordic Credit Rating, has today successfully issued NOK 750 million in new 5-year senior unsecured green bonds. The bond issue has a floating rate coupon of 3 months Nibor + 1.20% p.a.

An amount equal to the net proceeds from the green bond issuance will be used in accordance with Elopak’s Green Bond Framework, including inter alia refinancing of debt originally incurred to finance such green projects. The bonds will be swapped to EUR.

The settlement date is set to December 4, 2025, and Elopak will apply for the green bonds to be listed at Nordic ABM.

SEB acted as Sole Manager for the transaction.

data.title: Monte Paschi Says CEO Probed by Italian Prosecutors
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud

Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena said Chief Executive Luigi Lovaglio is being investigated by Italian prosecutors in a case related to allegations of supervisory obstruction and market manipulation.

The Italian lender said Thursday that it will fully cooperate with authorities and can provide all necessary information. Lovaglio declined to comment through a bank representative.

Monte Paschi said it received notification of a search warrant from Milan Public Prosecutor's Office and that a notice of investigation has been served on Lovaglio in his capacity as CEO. The bank said the probe relates to allegations of obstruction of supervisory functions and market manipulation, but didn't provide specifics.

Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com

data.title: Humilis Investment Strategies Announces Strategic Partnership as a Model Provider Within RBC Dominion Securities' Premier SMA Platform
data.description:
Milestone partnership validates Humilis' long-term equity approach and expands reach to Canada's high-net-worth investors

TORONTO, ON / ACCESS Newswire / November 27, 2025 / Humilis Investment Strategies (HiS) today announced its second significant milestone: a strategic partnership with RBC Dominion Securities Inc. (RBC DS) as a model provider for its Separately Managed Accounts (SMA) platform. This collaboration underscores Humilis' growing reputation for delivering consistent, process-driven investment performance and marks a strategic expansion of its Canadian distribution network. Humilis will bring its high-conviction global portfolios to RBC DS advisors and their discerning clients.

"Engaging with RBC DS, an institution synonymous with leadership, stability, and advisor excellence, marks an exciting next step in our expansion," said Brian Belski, CEO and Chief Investment Officer of Humilis Investment Strategies. "Our team has deep respect for RBC DS' commitment to putting clients first, and we are incredibly honoured that they have chosen us to be part of their excellent and professional investment platform. This is a tremendous milestone for Humilis and further reflects our long-standing dedication to Canadian investors."

The collaboration significantly enhances Humilis' distribution capabilities and positions the firm for accelerated growth in Canada, leveraging RBC's operational expertise to scale efficiently. Humilis expects to onboard RBC DS advisors in Q1 2026 and anticipates further platform expansions in Canada and the U.S. throughout the year.

Contact Information

Ryan Edwards

Portfolio Analyst

239-799-4680
